title: Graduate Data Analyst
snippet: Collaborate with data engineers to develop robust and reusable data models. Strong communication and data storytelling skills.
description: Company Overview Founded in 2014, and listed on the ASX since 2015, Superloop’s purpose is to enable better internet for Australian homes and businesses. We enable challenger retail brands (including Superloop and Exetel brands) to take a larger share of the market, leveraging Superloop’s Infrastructure-on-Demand platform. Superloop provides connectivity and services to customers in three segments of the market: Consumer, Business and Wholesale. Our offerings leverage Superloop’s investments in physical infrastructure assets that include fibre, subsea cables and fixed wireless, as well as Superloop’s software platforms. Hundreds of thousands of homes and businesses rely on Superloop and Exetel every day for their connectivity needs. Visit www.superloop.com to learn more. Role Purpose The Data and AI team is seeking a Graduate Data Analyst with a strong problem-solving mindset and a foundation in computer science, data, engineering, or a related quantitative discipline. This role is focused not only on delivering insights, but also on designing scalable, automated data and analytics solutions that create real business value. In this role, you will work closely with business stakeholders to understand problems, translate them into structured, data-driven approaches, and leverage modern platforms such as Databricks, along with AI and automation tools, to build efficient, reusable workflows and analytics products. You will act as a bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, taking ownership from requirements gathering through to solution design, implementation, optimisation, and continuous improvement. Key Responsibilities Partner with stakeholders to understand business problems and define data-driven solutions. Design and build scalable data and analytics solutions using Databricks. Develop automated pipelines, reports, and workflows to reduce manual effort. Apply AI and advanced analytics techniques where appropriate. Collaborate with data engineers to develop robust and reusable data models. Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. Continuously identify opportunities to automate and improve processes. Ensure strong data quality, governance, and adherence to best practices. Qualifications and Experience Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field. Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking skills. Programming skills in Python and SQL. Understanding of data structures and software engineering concepts is highly desirable. Exposure to Databricks or cloud-based data platforms is advantageous. Interest in automation, AI, and building scalable systems. Strong communication and data storytelling skills. Curiosity and willingness to learn new tools and technologies. On Offer Free Home Broadband Free Mobile SIM plan Flexibility – 3 days/week in the office Lunch on Wednesdays, snacks, donuts and more! Diverse and inclusive culture Gain skills and build relationships to succeed in the telco and tech industries, at Superloop and beyond. At Superloop, we’re dedicated to creating a supportive and inclusive workplace where everyone feels safe, valued, and empowered to be who they are. We actively embrace diversity and celebrate the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of our team members. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds to apply, regardless of gender, age, faith, ethnicity, nationality, sexuality, neurodiversity, or physical ability. We’re committed to ensuring that our hiring processes are accessible and inclusive for everyone interested in joining Superloop.

title: Data Analyst
snippet: Analyse discovery scan outputs, CMDB data , infrastructure inventories, and application registers to identify decommissioning opportunities and support technical…
description: Data Analyst (Health Manager Level 3) Turn complex needs into digital outcomes that improve healthcare – with hybrid work and growth opportunities Temporary Full Time Exempt for up to 12 months Hybrid flexibility for work-life balance | Chatswood, St Leonards or Charlestown Attractive salary, from $132,236.00 + 12% Super + 17.5% annual leave loading Applications Close: 11:59pm, Sunday 12th July 2026 Your work matters at eHealth NSW As the digital centre of excellence for NSW Health, we design and deliver secure, scalable technology that supports patient care across the state, helping clinicians provide better healthcare, now and into the future. Join eHealth NSW to create real-world impact, drive meaningful outcomes and support the health of millions every day. Learn more about us at eHealth NSW. Join the Team As a Data Analyst of our Application Consolidation and Decommissioning Program, you will be responsible for supporting a large-scale technology optimisation and application rationalisation program. This role will focus on identifying opportunities to retire legacy applications, infrastructure, and technology assets while supporting data-driven decision-making across multiple stakeholders. In this role, you will: Analyse discovery scan outputs, CMDB data, infrastructure inventories, and application registers to identify decommissioning opportunities and support technical assessments. Review application, server, database, and infrastructure information to develop recommendations and inform decision-making. Prepare reports, dashboards, presentations, and governance materials to communicate findings and progress. Engage with ICT teams, application owners, and stakeholders to validate data, assessment outcomes, and decommissioning activities. Support benefits reporting, hardware audits, asset reviews, data quality improvements, and application decommissioning initiatives. View the position description here. About You To be successful in this role you’ll need: Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently. Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and experience analysing large, complex datasets to identify trends and insights. Ability to interpret technical infrastructure, application, and asset management data, with knowledge of application lifecycle management and decommissioning processes highly regarded. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders and present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences. Experience with CMDBs, asset management or discovery tools, and reporting technologies such as Power BI, SQL, or PowerShell; experience in healthcare, government, or large enterprise ICT environments is advantageous. What We Offer At eHealth NSW, our benefits are designed to provide you with the flexibility, growth and support when you need it. We provide: Hybrid and flexible working options to support balance and productivity Allocation day off per month in addition to annual leave Salary packaging to maximise your take-home pay Learn More About Us Find out how we hire at eHealth NSW Check out our diversity and inclusion commitment How to Apply Submit your cover letter and most up to date resume (up to 5 pages), highlighting your relevant skills and experience. While we accept AI use - we want your application to feel genuine and true to you. For questions around the role or recruitment process, including adjustments, please contact our Talent Advisor or Hiring Manager, Jamie Lovick and quote REQ668928 . Important information This recruitment may be used to establish a Talent Pool for similar roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months. To be eligible for this role you must have current Australian work rights (Australian citizen, permanent resident, New Zealand citizen with a current passport, or hold a valid visa with permission to work in Australia). For this role 'Exempt' is refers to a fixed term contract of more than 13 weeks. If you currently reside outside NSW, please indicate in your application whether you are willing to relocate if successful. .

title: Data Analyst
snippet: In this role, you will engage with stakeholders to identify opportunities and challenges that can be addressed through data solutions, analyse data and…
description: Our team sits within the Risk Management Group Data Digital Design & Engineering division as part of the Data Delivery team, which partners with business stakeholders to analyse requirements and deliver data solutions aligned to business objectives and transformation programmes, working closely with Technology to design appropriate data architecture. Aligned to the Financial Risk portfolio, the role will focus on supporting the Credit Risk division in delivering data requirements and solutions driven by regulatory priorities, continuous improvement initiatives and new opportunities. We are a global financial services group operating in 30 markets with 57 years of unbroken profitability. At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that is fulfilling and creates value. You will bring your insights and expertise to the task at hand and feel supported as you make your own kind of impact for a better future. What role will you play? As a Data Business Analyst within the Data Delivery team, you will work closely with stakeholders to understand regulatory and internally driven business needs, define data deliverables, and conduct data analysis. You will translate business and data requirements into clear functional specifications, while collaborating with the broader data team to ensure alignment with established design patterns and architectural principles. You will also contribute to enhancing Macquarie’s data capabilities, with a particular focus on Credit Risk. In this role, you will engage with stakeholders to identify opportunities and challenges that can be addressed through data solutions, analyse data and modelling processes, and define data pipeline uplift initiatives. You will support solution design and data modelling, coordinate with the broader team to enable successful end-to-end delivery, and ensure adherence to data best practices, including architecture principles and data management frameworks. In addition, you will manage data-related risks and issues, propose remediation actions, and maintain strong stakeholder relationships across the Credit division and wider enterprise teams. What you offer 1-3 years Data analysis / analytics experience using SQL (Alteryx experience beneficial) or a similar role within financial services or risk management Strong abstract reasoning and problem solving skills - ability to define solutions to solve business problems Experience of embedding change/ End-to-End process uplift across business, data & technology will be beneficial Ability to gather requirements from stakeholders with a technical lens and mindset to conceptualise from the get-go Passionate about data and hold an inquisitive mindset with a genuine desire to understand and solve problem Organisational skills with experience in tools such as JIRA and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on employment type, include: 1 wellbeing leave day per year Up to 5 additional service bonus leave days per year Up to 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of transition leave upon return to work, and 6 weeks’ paid leave for non-primary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave and donation matching Up to 12 months’ gender affirmation leave, including 6 weeks’ paid leave Access to Employee Assistance Program and wellbeing benefits including skin and health checks, and flu vaccinations Access to a wide range of salary packaging options Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About the Risk Management Group Our Risk Management Group works as an independent, and centralised function, responsible for independent and objective review and challenge, oversight, monitoring and reporting in relation to Macquarie’s material risks. We are a global team that aims to manage the risks of today and anticipate the risks of tomorrow. Our divisions include compliance, credit, financial crime risk, market risk, operational risk, aggregate risk and prudential, and central. Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and welcome all identities, including race, ethnicity, cultural identity, nationality, gender (including gender identity or expression), age, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, parental, caregiving or family status, family and domestic violence status, neurodiversity, religion or belief, disability, or socio-economic background. We welcome further discussions on how you can feel included and belong at Macquarie as you progress through our recruitment process. To learn more, contact our team by clicking here. Our aim is to provide reasonable adjustments to individuals who may need support during the recruitment process and through working arrangements. If you require additional assistance, please let us know in the application process.

snippet: ; Advanced capability with reporting and data visualisation tools. ; Analyse marketing, media and business performance data to uncover actionable insights.

title: Junior Data Analyst/Scientist
snippet: Understand data structures and business and extract and integrate required data sources for analysis according to business requirements.
description: The International Institute of Data & Analytics (IDA) is a not-for-profit hi-tech organization with foci on data science and big data analytics innovation, education and training, and commercialization and services. IDA offers unique experiences and engagement models for bridging the gaps between academia and business, enabling industry and government `easy to do business with’ leading scientists and specialists in data & analytics, and building a platform for engagements and collaborations between outstanding researchers, practitioners and educators. In data science and big data analytics, the IDA creates enablers for: Leading the field of data science and analytics innovation, training and services by building think-tank and thought leadership, and next-generation of data scientists and professionals; transforming excellent research and innovation outcomes to actionable data science and analytics systems, tools, services and training for industry innovation and transformation; and building a flexible and engaging culture and platform to connect local, regional and global resources in data & analytics research and business towards developing world-class centre of excellence and research networks. You shall find the IDA team very self-motivated, high standard and forward thinking focused, and highly hands-on. Responsibilities Collaborate with team leader and senior data scientists, as well as clients and internal stakeholders to understand and document their business requirements, problems and objectives. Understand data structures and business and extract and integrate required data sources for analysis according to business requirements. Develop and evaluate data analytics models, algorithms, systems and solutions, including undertaking data extraction, pre-processing, modeling, evaluation, deployment, and maintenance. Collaborate with senior data scientists and team leader and clients to productise and pilot analytical models for production and insight per clients required. Undertake cost-benefit, forecasting and impact analysis by effective presentations of data-driven findings to clients and stakeholders. Document and communicate all relevant work and work outcomes. Qualifications Solid background, knowledge and capabilities in statistics, mathematics, computer science, informatics, computing, or closely relevant scientific field. Experienced in data-driven analytics hands-on experience with deep know-how of predictive modeling, machine learning and data mining, and relevant modeling approaches and algorithms. Demonstrated capabilities to develop and evaluate statistical analysis and modeling algorithms and apply these algorithms for data-driven solutions to problems in new domains. Demonstrated knowledge of SQL (data extraction and manipulation), expertise in SAS programming, and software development skills in one to several scripting languages (Python/R). Demonstrated capabilities to communicate and engage with a range of stakeholder groups and technical project teams. Demonstrated capabilities to work effectively as a member of a data science project team. Demonstrated experience for independent problem-solving and self-motivated innovation, education and services. Contact : Jinjiu.Li@idawise.com

title: Data Analyst
snippet: Exposure to enterprise or large-scale data environments. Strong understanding of data transformation, modelling, and relational databases.
description: Why join Stryker? Looking for a place that values your unique talents? Discover Stryker's award-winning culture. We are proud to offer you our total rewards package which includes bonuses, healthcare, insurance benefits, retirement programs, wellness programs, as well as service and performance awards – not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are location specific. Job description We are excited to be named one of the World’s Best Workplaces by Fortune Magazine! We are proud to offer you our total rewards package which includes bonuses, healthcare, insurance benefits, retirement programs, wellness programs, as well as service and performance awards – not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are location specific. We’re looking for a commercially minded and curious Data Analyst to join our Analytics team. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across the full analytics lifecycle, solving problems with data, and collaborating with stakeholders to deliver meaningful business outcomes. You’ll play a key role in supporting analytics initiatives, translating data into insights, and contributing to decisions that drive performance and value across the organisation. About the Analytics Team Our Analytics team is a trusted partner to the business, supporting strategic decision-making through high-quality insights, advanced analytics, and strong commercial thinking. We work across functions and divisions to solve business problems and translate data into actionable outcomes. For us, analytics isn’t just about numbers — it’s about enabling better decisions, stronger performance, and sustainable value. What You’ll Do Partner with the business - Work with non‑technical stakeholders to understand requirements, clarify questions, and deliver clear, actionable insights. Support end‑to‑end analytics initiatives - Contribute to analytics projects from problem definition through to delivery, ensuring outputs align with business priorities. Translate data into insights - Analyse datasets and develop reports, dashboards, and visualisations that support business decision-making. Apply commercial thinking - Help assess the impact of initiatives and contribute to identifying opportunities to drive value through data. Contribute to continuous improvement - Support improvements to analytics processes, data quality, and reporting practices. Collaborate across teams - Work closely with data, technology, and business teams to deliver analytics solutions and share knowledge. Who You Are Emerging analytics professional - You have solid experience in data analytics and are confident applying your technical skills to real business problems. A strong collaborator - You build effective relationships and enjoy working with stakeholders to solve problems and deliver value. Curious and analytical - You ask questions, explore data deeply, and challenge assumptions to uncover insights. Commercially aware - You understand how data connects to business performance and decision-making. Clear communicator - You can translate technical analysis into simple, meaningful insights for non‑technical audiences. Adaptable and proactive - You work well in changing environments and take ownership of your work. What You Bring Required Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar ~2–4 years’ experience in a data, analytics, or business intelligence role Proficiency in SQL and at least one analytics language (e.g. Python, R) Experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI or Tableau) Strong understanding of data transformation, modelling, and relational databases Exposure to data governance concepts such as definitions, lineage, and calculations Experience presenting insights to non‑technical stakeholders Preferred Experience using Microsoft Fabric and/or Databricks Exposure to ERP and/or CRM systems Experience contributing to analytics initiatives across functions (e.g. Sales, Marketing, Operations) Familiarity with statistical analysis, exploratory data analysis, or hypothesis‑driven approaches Experience using Microsoft Power Platform (e.g. Power Automate) Exposure to enterprise or large-scale data environments Why Join Us This is a great opportunity to grow your technical capability, build stakeholder influence, and contribute to meaningful data-driven decision-making. Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies and, together with its customers, is driven to make healthcare better. The company offers innovative products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology, Orthopaedics and Spine that help improve patient and healthcare outcomes. Alongside its customers around the world, Stryker impacts more than 150 million patients annually.

title: Junior Data Scientist
snippet: You will work directly under our senior data science leads to extract actionable insights from sprawling, unstructured customer data.
description: Junior Data Scientist — Dingdanh Software Labs Location: Sydney CBD (1 Castlereagh St) Position: Full-Time, Permanent Compensation: AUD 88,000 / year + super Dingdanh Software Labs is expanding our core analytics division in Sydney. We are looking for an analytical, inquisitive Junior Data Scientist to join our predictive modeling group. If you are passionate about moving beyond theoretical machine learning concepts and want to clean, model, and deploy real-world production models that influence active digital platforms, this is your opportunity. You will work directly under our senior data science leads to extract actionable insights from sprawling, unstructured customer data. What Your Weeks Will Look Like: You will spend your time diving deep into our raw data lakes, cleaning messy datasets, and building predictive features. You will write robust scripts to handle data preprocessing, run exploratory data analysis (EDA) to spot hidden behavioral trends, and help train and tune machine learning models. A significant portion of your role involves verifying model accuracy, tracking down variance anomalies, and translating raw statistical outputs into clear visualizations for our product teams. You will also assist in setting up automated data verification pipelines to monitor production model drift over time. The Foundational Tooling You Need: Deep mathematical or statistical curiosity paired with practical experience writing clean data processing scripts in Python or R. Practical familiarity with core data science libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-Learn, or statistical equivalents. Functional capability writing SQL queries to pull, filter, and aggregate multi-table datasets out of relational databases. A highly structured approach to validating data assumptions, checking edge cases, and verifying model performance metrics. Candidates must possess valid, long-term working rights within Australia at the point of submitting their application. How to Apply: Please forward your current CV to our recruitment team. If you have a GitHub link, a Kaggle profile, or a write-up of a personal data project you engineered from scratch, please include it in your submission. Pay: $88,000.00 per year Benefits: Dental insurance Housing provided Maternity leave Salary packaging Work Location: In person

title: Junior Data Scientist
snippet: Minimum 1 year of relevant experience in data science, data analytics, BI or data engineering. Experience with ETL pipelines, data cleaning and data quality…
description: About our company: FMCG Analytics is a data-driven analytics and consulting business specialising in insights, forecasting, and performance analysis for the fast-moving consumer goods sector. The company supports clients in understanding customer behaviour, market trends, product performance, sales patterns, and operational efficiency through advanced data analysis and reporting. With continued growth in demand for data-led decision-making across the FMCG industry, we are seeking a Junior Data Scientist to join our team and contribute to the development of high-quality analytical solutions. Salary Range: $85,000 to $95,000 plus superannuation (depending upon experience & skills) Role: Full time Location: Drummoyne, NSW 2047 Main responsibilities Analyse FMCG, retail, sales, product and customer datasets to identify trends, patterns, market movements and business insights. Clean, transform and prepare data from databases, client platforms, websites, spreadsheets and reporting systems for analysis and dashboard reporting. Build, maintain and improve ETL pipelines and automated data workflows to support regular reporting, data refreshes and client-specific analysis. Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and visual reports that present sales performance, market trends, competitor insights, customer behaviour and KPI summaries. Use SQL to extract, join, filter and optimise datasets from relational databases, including preparing data for reporting and visualisation. Use Python and data analysis libraries such as Pandas and NumPy to clean, process and structure raw or unstructured data. Conduct data quality checks to ensure reports, dashboards and client outputs are accurate, consistent and reliable. Prepare drill-down reports and dashboards that allow stakeholders to review high-level trends as well as detailed client, product or market-level insights. Assist with automating recurring reporting tasks, including data collection, scheduled scripts, web scraping and report generation. Support management, customer success and client-facing teams by providing insights that assist with reporting, client engagement and retention strategies. Prepare client-specific data outputs, KPI summaries and Excel-based reports to meet business and stakeholder requirements. Document data sources, reporting logic, data preparation steps and dashboard processes to support transparency and repeatability. Contribute to the ongoing improvement of dashboards, data pipelines, reporting structures and analytics workflows across the business. Qualifications & Skills Master of Data Science or equivalent qualification. Minimum 1 year of relevant experience in data science, data analytics, BI or data engineering. Experience with Power BI dashboards and data visualisation. Strong SQL and Python skills, including Pandas and NumPy. Experience with ETL pipelines, data cleaning and data quality checks. Knowledge of databases such as PostgreSQL and cloud/data tools such as Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, DBT or Snowflake is desirable. Understanding of FMCG, retail, sales or market analytics will be advantageous. Strong problem-solving, attention to detail and communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a growing analytics-focused business. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Australian Citizens/ Permanent Residents preferred. Application Questions Please answer the following when submitting your application: · Do you have full Australian working rights? · Do you hold a Master of Data Science or equivalent qualification? · How many years of experience do you have in data science, business analytics, or a similar analytical role? · Do you have experience using Power BI or similar dashboard/reporting tools? · Do you have experience using SQL and Python for data cleaning, transformation and analysis? · Do you have experience building or supporting ETL pipelines and automated data workflows? · Do you have experience working with FMCG, retail, sales, customer or market-related datasets? Pay: $85,000.00 – $95,000.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Insights Analyst
snippet: Turn data into actionable insights. You're a confident, self-directed analyst who loves uncovering the story behind the data.
description: Date: 1 Jul 2026 Turn data into actionable insights Permanent full-time Opportunity | $190,026 + 16.5% Super + Bonus Based at our Head Office in Haymarket, Sydney | Hybrid Work Environment Receive a Monthly Day Off in support of work life balance! About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good We’re the largest distributor of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all. It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals. Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds. See yourself working here As an Insight Analyst in our Business Insights team, you'll own the dashboards and reporting that leaders and decision-makers rely on every day — combining commercial understanding of how Ausgrid works with strong technical craft to deliver insight that's accurate, fast and genuinely trusted. Your key responsibilities will include: Designing, developing and maintaining interactive Power BI dashboards Providing strategic advice, ad-hoc analysis and special-purpose reporting across all levels of the business Partnering with Insights Leads to keep the reporting framework fit for purpose and future-proofed for business needs. Liaising with the business to understand the questions they're trying to answer Maintaining clear documentation and explainability to build trust and reduce single-point dependency. Updating corporate, board and regulatory reporting outputs accurately and on time. What you bring to the role You're a confident, self-directed analyst who loves uncovering the story behind the data. You combine strong technical and modelling skills with genuine curiosity and initiative — pursuing and resolving challenging issues with limited direction. Just as importantly, you build trusted relationships, communicate clearly, and bring stakeholders along with you. To be successful, you will have: A degree in Finance, Economics or a commercial discipline, OR in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics or Engineering. Extensive experience within a large, data-intensive organisation. Strong technical and modelling skills across complex data environments. Demonstrable experience delivering on strategic imperatives using data analytics. Established interpersonal skills, with experience engaging a variety of internal and external stakeholders. A high degree of initiative and confidence, with the ability to resolve challenging issues independently. Demonstrated experience building and publishing Power BI dashboards. Life at Ausgrid At Ausgrid, we go beyond just a competitive salary to offer an exciting range of benefits. From salary sacrificing electric vehicles and novated leasing, to discounted corporate health insurance and access to Fitness Passport, our benefits support your work here, and your life out there. Celebrate success every day with our employee recognition program to bring out each other’s best. Looking to grow professionally? We’ve got you covered with study assistance, secondments, training and mentors, whose advice you’ll keep wherever you go. We’re proud to offer gender-neutral parental leave to balance work when growing your family. And if you’re caring for elderly parents, our aged care support services offer expert guidance to help you access care for your loved ones. At Ausgrid, it's all about supporting you in every aspect of life. If you require support with your application/reasonable adjustments for the interview process, please reach out to our dedicated Talent Team recruitment@ausgrid.com.au. Application close date: Friday 24th July (please note, this date is subject to early closure) Applicants must have full Australian work rights to apply.

title: Shareholder Analyst
snippet: Collect, analyse, and interpret data on shareholder activities and market trends. Based in Sydney’s CBD with flexible hybrid working, this role plays a key part…
description: Location: Sydney CBD | Hybrid Working Model Join a global leader in investor relations and financial services Make your mark in financial markets and analytics We’re seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Shareholder Analyst to join MUFG’s Investor Relations (IR) team. Based in Sydney’s CBD with flexible hybrid working, this role plays a key part in compiling and analysing shareholder data to support strategic decision-making, ensure compliance, and provide meaningful insights to listed company clients. What you’ll do As a Shareholder Analyst, you will: Collect, analyse, and interpret data on shareholder activities and market trends Prepare comprehensive reports on changes in share ownership and fund activity Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders to gather shareholder intelligence Monitor shareholder movements to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements Support process improvements to enhance efficiency across the analysis team Respond to client queries and assist with documentation and reporting Contribute to the delivery of high-quality services across the business What we’re looking for This is a great opportunity for a recent graduate or someone early in their career looking to build a strong foundation in financial services and analytics. While prior experience in a corporate environment is preferred, we’re equally focused on finding someone with the right mindset and eagerness to learn. You’ll thrive in this role if you have: A genuine interest in equity markets and a willingness to learn Some understanding of investor relations or financial markets (preferred but not essential – training will be provided) Intermediate Microsoft Office skills (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) – our core system is in-house, and you’ll receive full training Excellent attention to detail and time management skills Clear and confident written and verbal communication The ability to multitask, manage competing priorities, and stay organised A collaborative, proactive, and self-motivated approach Fluency in Mandarin and/or Cantonese Why join MUFG Pension & Market Services? We’re proud to offer a supportive, inclusive, and flexible working environment where your wellbeing and growth matter. Our hybrid working model provides the balance to thrive professionally and personally. You’ll also benefit from: Competitive remuneration and salary packaging options Paid parental leave Health insurance discounts Volunteer and wellbeing leave Ongoing learning and development opportunities The MUFG Way MUFG is one of the world’s leading financial groups. At MUFG Pension & Market Services, we follow The MUFG Way —a commitment to integrity, responsibility, and sustainable growth. Our values of professionalism, teamwork, and inclusion guide everything we do. Apply now If you're ready to grow your career in financial services and make an impact through data and insight, we’d love to hear from you. Please note: Applicants must have permanent working rights in Australia. MUFG Pension & Market Services is a global, digitally enabled business that empowers a brighter future by connecting millions of people with their assets – safely, securely and responsibly. Through our two businesses MUFG Retirement Solutions and MUFG Corporate Markets, we partner with a diversified portfolio of global clients to provide robust, efficient and scalable services, purpose-built solutions and modern technology platforms that deliver world class outcomes and experiences. A member of MUFG, a global financial group, we help manage regulatory complexity, improve data management and connect people with their assets, through exceptional user experience that leverages the expertise of our people combined with scalable technology, digital connectivity and data insights. Our MUFG Corporate Markets division provides clients with a comprehensive corporate market offering that connects issuers to their stakeholders. Our uniquely integrated range of corporate markets capabilities includes shareholder management and analytics, stakeholder engagement, share and unit registry, employee share plans, and digital and print communications. We also offer company secretarial support, as well as various specialist offerings such as insolvency solutions. We operate in twelve countries throughout Australasia, Asia, Africa, the Middle East, United Kingdom and Europe. Our teams combine a wealth of industry experience with a rigorous focus on client management to deliver a premium service underpinned by market leading digital technology provided by our Technology division. MUFG Pension & Market Services is building a dynamic, client focused, caring and inclusive culture that is built on the foundations of an entrepreneurial spirit, effective risk management, empathy and trust, and underpinned by its core values. We are an inclusive employer whose people work collaboratively. We encourage, support and value the various talents and perspectives of our people and promote a flexible and blended work environment where our people can thrive and their wellbeing is supported. We know that diversity drives better client outcomes, continuous improvement, and growth. Be part of the MUFG Pension & Market Services journey and together we will achieve our full potential. We treat all individuals fairly and equitably and do not discriminate on the basis of diverse characteristics including, but not limited to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, cultural background, physical abilities/disabilities, religious or political belief, marital or family status or carers responsibilities. Candidates must have the relevant work rights to be considered for an opportunity at MUFG Pension & Market Services. Successful applicants will be required to complete background screening prior to commencement of employment.

title: Data and Workforce Analyst
snippet: Strong data analytics and business analysis capability. Ability to translate data into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders.
description: Primary Details Time Type: Full time Worker Type: Employee Location: National, Flexible Type: full time, 12-month fixed term contract Hybrid role, Happy to talk flexible working The opportunity The Data and Workforce Analyst position sits within the AUSPAC division and plays a key role in supporting strategic workforce planning and value-based design initiatives. You’ll use data and analytics to provide forward-looking insights, helping the business understand workforce cost, structure and decision-making in the context of future trends, including the impact of AI. About QBE At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch. We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner. What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind. Your new role In this role, you’ll operate at a high level across the business, using data to unpack how workforce decisions are made and where value can be optimised. You’ll partner closely with stakeholders across HR, Finance and the business to deliver insight-led analysis that supports planning, cost transparency and future-focused workforce strategy. Key responsibilities include: Supporting demand and supply forecasting, scenario modelling and sensitivity analysis across workforce planning Owning and managing workforce data, ensuring integrity, consistency and auditability of key inputs Translating complex workforce and business data into clear, decision-ready insights Leading data analysis to support value-based design (VBD) initiatives and business case development Partnering with Finance and Reward to align workforce cost, productivity and activity assumptions Building scalable, repeatable analytics solutions to reduce reliance on ad-hoc reporting About you You’re a commercially minded data professional who enjoys getting into the detail while keeping sight of the bigger picture. You’re curious about how organisations make decisions, comfortable challenging assumptions, and interested in how trends like AI will shape the future of work. Key requirements: Strong data analytics and business analysis capability Experience working with large, complex datasets (ideally workforce or people data), with solid SQL skills Experience with Workday or similar HCM systems (highly regarded) Demonstrated track record in strategic workforce planning and analytics, with Adaptive Planning exposure an advantage Ability to translate data into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders Strong stakeholder engagement skills Experience supporting project delivery, exposure to org design work, and comfortable working across multiple priorities would be advantageous Benefits We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from: ‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance Life Leave, supporting the life events we all experience, leaving your annual leave for rest 18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super A range of discounts on insurance products, car hire, hotels, goods and services To learn more about benefits of working with us, click here. Rewards & Recognition We value our employee’s experience with us and are proud to have been recognised for the following awards: 2025 Winner of Excellence in Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Award at the Insurance Business Australia Awards for our Respect@QBE program Platinum employer on the Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI) – the definitive national benchmark on LGBTQI+ workplace inclusion Ranked in the top ten in the AAGE Top Graduate Employers 2025 for medium sized program as voted by graduates APPLY NOW and let’s make it happen! If you’re looking for a career that combines your expertise and your empathy, click Apply today. Your career. At the heart of it. Successful applicants will need to complete work rights, police and credit checks through our online provider as a condition of employment. QBE recognise the value of diverse perspectives and experience. We are committed to an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected for who they are. If we can provide support with access requirements, alternative work arrangements or you would like to connect with one of our networks please contact us at recruitment@qbe.com Skills: Analytical Thinking, Business Intelligence (BI), Business Intelligence Applications, Critical Thinking, Data Analysis, Data Analytics, Data Modeling, Decision Making, Intentional collaboration, Managing performance, Process Improvements, Quality Management, Reporting and Analysis, Risk Management, Stakeholder Management Application Close Date: 04/07/2026 11:59 PM How to Apply: To submit your application, click "Apply" and follow the step by step process. title: Data Management | Executive Analyst
snippet: We partner within the division to embed best practice data management, improve data quality, and enable informed, data-driven decision making.
description: The Financial Management, People and Engagement Group Data Office plays a central role in connecting business, data, and technology to deliver scalable data governance across a complex enterprise environment. We partner within the division to embed best practice data management, improve data quality, and enable informed, data-driven decision making. We are a global financial services group operating in 30 markets with 57 years of unbroken profitability. At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that is fulfilling and creates value. You will bring your insights and expertise to the task at hand and feel supported as you make your own kind of impact for a better future. What role will you play? As an Executive Analyst, Data Management, you will support the development and uplift of the Group’s data management capabilities across a range of disciplines, including governance, data quality, controls, ownership, and risk management. A key focus in your role will be creating and maintaining metrics that track maturity, risk, control effectiveness, and delivery progress, while also contributing to the implementation of frameworks, standards, and processes that strengthen data management outcomes. You will analyse information, identify trends and gaps, prepare reporting and insights for stakeholders and leadership, and work closely with subject matter experts and divisional teams to support practical and consistent adoption of data management practices. What you offer Experience in data management, governance, risk, analytics, or consulting within financial services, with a broad understanding of core data management disciplines and how they are applied in practice. Strong analytical capability, including experience developing metrics, dashboards, reporting, or performance insights to support governance, decision-making, and continuous improvement. Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline, with strong problem-solving skills and the ability to turn complex information into clear, actionable insights. Some experience supporting the implementation of data management frameworks, standards, controls, or governance activities, with strong attention to detail and a practical delivery mindset. Confidence working with stakeholders across teams to gather requirements, support implementation, validate measures, and embed consistent data management practices. Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to prepare clear reporting and present insights in a way that supports action and accountability. A proactive and curious mindset, with the ability to identify opportunities to improve metrics, reporting, processes, and broader data management outcomes over time. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on employment type, include: 1 wellbeing leave day per year Up to 5 additional service bonus leave days per year Up to 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of transition leave upon return to work, and 6 weeks’ paid leave for non-primary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave and donation matching Up to 12 months’ gender affirmation leave, including 6 weeks’ paid leave Access to Employee Assistance Program and wellbeing benefits including skin and health checks, and flu vaccinations Access to a wide range of salary packaging options Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About Financial Management, People and Engagement Financial Management, People and Engagement (FPE) provides a single interface for Macquarie’s businesses across key areas of people, strategy, communications and financial management. Comprising two pillars – Financial Management, and People and Engagement – it is responsible for managing the Group’s financial, tax and treasury activities and strategic priorities, fostering our culture through people and community engagement strategies, and engaging with stakeholders to protect and promote Macquarie’s reputation globally. Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and welcome all identities, including race, ethnicity, cultural identity, nationality, gender (including gender identity or expression), age, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, parental, caregiving or family status, family and domestic violence status, neurodiversity, religion or belief, disability, or socio-economic background. title: Data Analyst, Risk
snippet: Experience with big data platforms and related tools is a plus. Strong background in data analytics, user behavior analysis, and customer segmentation.
description: Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world. About the Role We are looking for a highly motivated and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for building and maintaining user tagging systems, developing user profiles, and generating actionable insights from large-scale user data. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to support personalized services, risk control strategies, and data-driven decision-making across the organization. Responsibilities Manage and maintain a comprehensive user tagging system to support personalized services and risk control initiatives. Analyze user behavior data to build accurate, dynamic, and actionable user profiles. Perform data mining, feature engineering, and exploratory analysis to extract meaningful insights from large-scale datasets. Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including Product, Risk, Marketing, and Technology, to optimize user segmentation and targeting strategies. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of user tags and user profiles, continuously improving data quality, accuracy, and relevance. Support the implementation of data-driven decision-making processes across the organization. Identify patterns, anomalies, and emerging trends in user behavior to support business growth and risk mitigation. Contribute to the continuous improvement of data methodologies, profiling frameworks, and analytical processes. Requirements Bachelor’s degree or above in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field. Minimum 3–5 years of experience in data analysis, user profiling, customer segmentation, or related areas. Strong background in data analytics, user behavior analysis, and customer segmentation. Proficiency in Python, SQL , and data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI. Experience with big data platforms and related tools is a plus. Solid understanding of user tagging methodologies and user journey mapping. Familiarity with black-market ecosystems and the operating models of bonus abuse, promotion abuse, or fraudulent studio groups is highly preferred; hands-on experience in identifying and combating such activities is a strong advantage. Knowledge of machine learning techniques and experience applying them to user data is an advantage. Excellent analytical thinking and problem-solving skills. Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, international team environment. Detail-oriented, with a strong sense of data accuracy, consistency, and integrity. Experience with AI technologies and practical applications is a plus. Preferred Qualifications Experience in internet platforms, fintech, risk control, growth analytics, or anti-fraud related domains. Familiarity with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, and user lifecycle analysis. Experience working with large-scale behavioral datasets and real-time analytics scenarios. Why Binance Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem Collaborate with world-class talent in a user-centric global organization with a flat structure Tackle unique, fast-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment Thrive in a results-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning Competitive salary and company benefits Work-from-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team) Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success. By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our Candidate Privacy Notice . We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
